name: Torrent Manager
short_description: Python application for managing torrent clients (rTorrent, Transmission) via secure REST API with authentication, CLI, and web frontend.
long_description: Torrent Manager is a comprehensive Python application that provides unified management of torrent client instances (rTorrent and Transmission) through a secure REST API, command-line interface, and web frontend. It automates common torrent operations including moving completed files, managing seeding duration, logging activity, and transferring downloaded content via rsync over SSH. The system features secure session-based and API key authentication, background polling services with circuit breaker error handling, automatic media identification and metadata retrieval from TMDB/IMDB, lifecycle callbacks for custom event handling, RSS feed automation, and HLS media streaming integration. Built on FastAPI with SQLite database via Peewee ORM, it offers both programmatic and interactive interfaces for torrent management.

modules.description: FastAPI application entry point with background services (seeding monitor, torrent poller, transfer service, media streaming worker).
RTorrent XMLRPC client with error handling, target fallback, and comprehensive timeout support for rTorrent servers.
Transmission RPC client implementing the same interface as RTorrentClient for transparent interchangeable use.
Abstract base class defining the common torrent client interface for rTorrent and Transmission implementations.
Session-based and API key authentication system with sliding expiration, remember-me tokens, and bcrypt password hashing.
Background polling service that fetches torrent status from servers with circuit breaker error handling and intelligent interval adjustment.
Transfer service managing rsync-based file downloads from remote servers with retry logic, progress parsing, and auto-deletion support.
Automatic media identification service using torrent_match and media_metadata for TMDB/IMDB metadata retrieval and Jellyfin NFO generation.
Torrent lifecycle callback system allowing custom Python scripts to respond to events (added, started, stopped, completed, removed, error, transfer_*)
Peewee database models for users, sessions, servers, torrents, transfers, metadata, and RSS feeds.
REST API endpoints for torrent operations: list, add, start, stop, remove, and metadata management.
REST API endpoints for user registration, login, logout, session management, and API key creation/revocation.
Command-line interface providing terminal access to all API functionality for user authentication, server management, and torrent operations.
Server startup and configuration with environment variable handling, database initialization, and background service startup.
RSS feed automation service for monitoring feeds and automatically adding torrents matching configured rules.
